finding: handlers.rs move is mechanical-only: diff HEAD vs moved copy = 84 lines, all import-path / pub(crate)->pub / super::->crate::auth:: / <R>-generic / debug_handler-removal. No logic, string, or response-shape change.
OpenAPI byte-identity on BOTH surfaces: types.ui.ts + types.desktop.ts BYTE-IDENTICAL; openapi.ui.json + openapi.desktop.json CANONICALLY-EQUAL vs baseline. The N2 hard gate.

OAuth redirect_uri still derives ONLY from request headers gated by trust_forwarded_headers (the SECURITY comment block at oauth_authorize preserved verbatim); the user-supplied ?redirect_uri= is still ignored. No weakening of the SSRF/redirect posture in the move.
evidence: handlers.rs oauth_authorize body unchanged; grep trust_forwarded_headers

finding: No secret/JWT/config global leaks into the SDK: moved handlers name zero crate::core/Config/Repos/secret/url_validator. Secrets reach provider CRUD only via the already-extracted ctx (AuthContext) + provider repository. token_response cookie-mode shaping (httpOnly refresh) preserved byte-for-byte.

Permission gating preserved: every admin handler keeps RequirePermissions<R,(AuthProvidersManage|Read,)>; session-settings keeps SessionSettings{Read,Manage}; profile keeps ProfileEdit. Const permission strings byte-identical, so wire enforcement + 403 schema unchanged. Public endpoints (oauth/providers/link-account) remain intentionally ungated.
evidence: grep RequirePermissions + permissions const strings; admin_providers_test 403 test green

finding: Handlers emit only crate-local AuthSyncEntity/AuthSyncAction via the injected AuthSyncSink; Audience/SyncOrigin from ziee_framework::sync. Zero ziee-domain SyncEntity variant crosses into the SDK crate. admin_providers sync-emission tests (create_auth_provider_emits_sync_to_admins_only etc.) pass.
evidence: grep AuthSyncEntity in http/*.rs; admin_providers_test 10/10 incl sync tests

finding: Route paths + HTTP methods + operationIds unchanged (auth_routes/auth_admin_routes builders carry the same api_route/route calls in the same order; oauth authorize=GET, callback=GET+POST). operationIds are literal strings in the moved *_docs.
evidence: routes.rs diff; operationId grep vs baseline unchanged (golden)

finding: routes feature default-on, aide/axum optional; the routes surface has 0 query! macros so the auth-only build-DB (ziee_auth_build_<key>) is unchanged. Framework crates + E11 skeleton build framework-only WITHOUT a build DB (skeleton does not depend on ziee-auth).
evidence: cargo check -p skeleton-server no DATABASE_URL = 0; grep -c query! http/*.rs = 0

finding: All external consumers keep resolving via module-path shims: crate::modules::auth::{handlers::{token_response,ensure_unique_username}, jwt_extractor::{JwtAuth,OptionalJwtAuth}, permissions::*, session_settings::*}; crate::modules::user::permissions::{ProfileRead,ProfileEdit}. app/handlers, onboarding, hub, sync, chat, lib.rs all compile unchanged.
evidence: cargo check -p ziee = 0; grep of the 6 external ref sites

finding: Route bundle generic over R: IdentityResolver<User=User,Group=Group> — auth mechanism pluggable (N1), User/Group fixed to the crate's own wire types. ziee mounts ZieeIdentityResolver; a second app supplies its own. Non-permission handlers stay non-generic. #[debug_handler] dropped from generic handlers (compile-time-only, no runtime/OpenAPI effect).
evidence: routes.rs builders + 10 generic handler sigs; ziee register_routes mounts ::<ZieeIdentityResolver>()

finding: ziee-desktop builds clean (exit 0) and its golden (types.desktop.ts + openapi.desktop.json) is byte/canonical-identical. The desktop tunnel_auth crate's own change_password is untouched (separate route).
evidence: cargo check -p ziee-desktop = 0; golden desktop identical

finding: No new warnings introduced (ziee warning set == baseline: KB_MAX_DOCUMENTS_DEFAULT, is_active, is_unread, list_enabled_for_health_check + the informational build-DB note). Shim re-exports carry #[allow(unused_imports)] to stay clean when a consumer names only a subset.
evidence: cargo check -p ziee warning diff vs baseline

finding: Moved-surface integration tests pass with my change: auth::admin_providers_test 10/10 (provider CRUD + test-config + 403-gate + sync-emit), and the full server boots successfully with the moved routes mounted. Full auth suite blocked ONLY by two PRE-EXISTING MIGRATE-squash harness gaps (I touched zero test/harness files).
evidence: auth-focus2 log: test result ok 10 passed 0 failed; server 'started successfully' log line
